items.title: Sigmoid
items.description: Sigmoid maps to (0, 1) with center 0.5; tanh maps to (−1, 1) with center 0. Sigmoid details and binary-output use cases live in Sigmoid — this topic owns the canonical tanh vs sigmoid comparison.

items.title: ReLU and Variants
items.description: Modern deep feedforward networks prefer ReLU-family activations to avoid saturation and vanishing gradients. Tanh remains relevant where bounded, zero-centered states matter (especially recurrence); ReLU and Variants covers when to switch.

items.title: Recurrent Cells
items.description: Classic RNN hidden updates h_t = tanh(·) use tanh for bounded, zero-centered state vectors. Recurrent Cells explains the full recurrence; this topic supplies the activation properties.

items.title: LSTM Gates
items.description: LSTMs combine sigmoid gates (0–1) with tanh on the candidate cell state. LSTM Gates covers gate mechanics; tanh's role on the cell candidate is defined here.
